page: Microsoft Azure B2pts_v2 is a burstable virtual machine belonging to the Bpsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an ARM64 architecture powered by an Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor. This processor provides 2 physical cores and 2 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.0 GHz, with a cache configuration of 64 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 1.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 0.5 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, though it is premium storage capable.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the B2pts_v2 is positioned at the lower end of the performance spectrum. In stress-ng testing, single-core CPU performance is average at 1822.09, but multi-core performance drops to the bottom 25% at 3611.98. Memory bandwidth is highly constrained, with read-only small block cached bandwidth at 140.98 GB/sec and large block uncached read and write speeds at 27.09 GB/sec and 16.47 GB/sec respectively. Passmark metrics reflect similar limitations, with a CPU score of 1605.25 and a memory score of 829.4, both falling into the bottom 10%. Redis SET operations achieve 1.43 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput is measured at 1.55 GB/sec.

The B2pts_v2 offers a highly cost-efficient entry point for lightweight workloads due to its burstable CPU allocation model and minimal resource footprint. However, the primary tradeoff is the extremely limited memory capacity of 1.0 GB and the lack of local storage, which restricts its utility for memory-intensive applications or heavy database operations. This server is best suited for low-traffic static web serving, minor background tasks, development testing, or lightweight microservices that can tolerate burstable CPU performance and operate within strict memory limits.
description: Microsoft Azure B2pts_v2 is an ARM64-based burstable virtual machine from the Bpsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Powered by an Ampere ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor, it features 2 physical cores and 2 vCPUs clocked at 3.0 GHz, paired with 1.0 GB of system memory. The server does not include local storage or GPUs, but supports premium storage. Benchmark data indicates modest performance, with average single-core stress-ng results but weak multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics, including a Passmark CPU score of 1605.25 and a memory score of 829.4. With its burstable CPU allocation and low resource density, this server provides a highly cost-efficient profile for lightweight, non-resource-intensive workloads. It is best suited for basic tasks such as low-volume static web serving, microservices, and development environments that operate within strict memory constraints.
og_description: Microsoft Azure B2pts_v2 is an ARM64 burstable server with 2 vCPUs and 1.0 GB RAM. Powered by an Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor, it offers a cost-efficient profile for lightweight utility workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure B2pts_v2 is an ARM64 burstable server featuring 2 vCPUs, 1.0 GB RAM, and an Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor for lightweight workloads.
tagline: A highly cost-efficient, burstable ARM64 virtual machine designed for lightweight utility tasks and development environments with minimal memory requirements.
bullet_points: ARM64 architecture powered by a 2-core Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor at 3.0 GHz
Burstable CPU allocation with 1.0 GB of system memory
No local storage included, but premium storage capable
Average single-core performance with constrained multi-core and memory bandwidth
Best suited for lightweight microservices, static web serving, and development testing
